welcome_to_the_art_design_studio: 🎨 Welcome to the Art & Design Studio!
explore_15_interactive_tools_that_teac: Explore 15 interactive tools that teach color theory, mathematical art, generative design, and visual accessibility. Each tab is a different creative canvas. Here’s what you can create:
mix_paints_with_subtractive_color_theo: Mix paints with subtractive color theory
create_pixel_art_on_a_grid_canvas: Create pixel art on a grid canvas
repeating_tile_patterns_like_m_c_esche: Repeating tile patterns like M.C. Escher
design_and_export_css_gradient_pattern: Design and export CSS gradient patterns
harmony_desc: Explore musical consonance, intervals, and visual sound relationships
color_theory_additive_vs_subtractive_m: Color theory (additive vs subtractive mixing, complementary colors, HSL/RGB), mathematical curves (hypotrochoids, Lissajous), fractals & self-similarity, tessellation geometry, op art visual perception, WCAG accessibility standards, and computational art. Every tool teaches the math behind the beauty.
hue_0_360_around_the_wheel_saturation_: Hue (0-360 around the wheel), saturation (purity), lightness (brightness). Complementary across, analogous adjacent, triadic 120° apart. Newton put the spectrum on a wheel in 1666.
paint_and_print_subtractive_cmy_mixes_: Paint and print = subtractive (CMY mixes to dark); light and screens = additive (RGB mixes to white). Same world, completely different math — a printer thinks in K plates, a TV thinks in Hz.
pixel_art_bitmap_craft_at_8_8_to_32_32: Pixel Art — bitmap craft at 8×8 to 32×32
each_pixel_is_a_deliberate_decision_ne: Each pixel is a deliberate decision. NES sprites famously fit a hero into 16×16 with a 4-color palette. Bresenham’s line algorithm draws diagonals without floats.
bilateral_mirror_rotational_n_fold_poi: Bilateral (mirror), rotational (n-fold), point. The 17 wallpaper groups classify every possible repeating 2D pattern — Escher’s entire body of work.
a_small_circle_rolls_inside_a_big_one_: A small circle rolls inside a big one, pen offset from center. Ratio of radii determines petal count; offset sets thickness. Toy patented 1965, math from 1700s.
sol_lewitt_wrote_instructions_the_wall: Sol LeWitt wrote instructions; the wall installer was the executor. Today: Processing, p5.js, Cinder. “The artist is the rule, not the result.”
spin_art_centripetal_physics_in_paint: Spin Art — centripetal physics in paint
drop_paint_spin_watch_it_fling_outward: Drop paint, spin, watch it fling outward in spirals. Damien Hirst made millions selling spin paintings. Same physics as a salad spinner; F = mω²r.
string_art_curves_from_straight_lines: String Art — curves from straight lines
connect_every_n_th_nail_an_envelope_cu: Connect every n-th nail; an envelope curve emerges. Mary Everest Boole introduced this as classroom math c. 1900. The straight-line cardioid is still hypnotic.
op_art_fooling_the_visual_system: Op Art — fooling the visual system
bridget_riley_s_moir_fields_vasarely_s: Bridget Riley’s moiré fields, Vasarely’s grids. The brain’s motion-detection edge cells over-fire on rapidly alternating contrast — the page appears to *vibrate*.
every_periodic_2d_tiling_fits_one_of_1: Every periodic 2D tiling fits one of 17 symmetry groups. Escher figured this out by visiting the Alhambra in 1936; he then spent 30 years exhausting the catalogue.
mandelbrot_1975_cauliflower_coastlines: Mandelbrot 1975. Cauliflower, coastlines, blood vessels, lightning, lung alveoli — all fractal. “Clouds are not spheres, mountains are not cones, bark is not smooth.”
css_gives_you_linear_radial_and_conic_: CSS gives you linear, radial, and conic gradients. Real rainbows have continuous spectra (no discrete bands) — the 7 “colors of the rainbow” were Newton’s arbitrary choice for musical reasons.
90s_magic_eye_craze_each_eye_sees_a_sl: 90s Magic Eye craze. Each eye sees a slightly shifted version; if you cross or diverge correctly, the brain fuses them into depth. ~5% of people genuinely can’t — not their fault.
wcag_2_1_normal_text_4_5_1_large_3_1_w: WCAG 2.1: normal text 4.5:1, large 3:1. Why low contrast hurts low-vision readers, even if you can read it. APCA (the WCAG 3.0 successor) uses perceptual lightness, not raw luminance ratio.
harmony_lab_hint: Compare consonant and dissonant intervals, connect frequency ratios to pattern, and translate musical relationships into visual harmony.
click_the_wheel_to_pick_a_hue: Click the wheel to pick a hue
repeats_a_pattern_around_a_central_poi: repeats a pattern around a central point. In
starfish_5_fold_snowflakes_6_fold_and_: , starfish (5-fold), snowflakes (6-fold), and flowers show this everywhere.
mandala_art_rose_windows_in_cathedrals: Mandala art, rose windows in cathedrals.
islamic_geometric_art_uses_radial_symm: Islamic geometric art uses radial symmetry extensively. Celtic knots, Navajo textiles, and Japanese family crests (“mon”) all rely on rotational symmetry.
uses_bilateral_reflection_symmetry_the: uses bilateral (reflection) symmetry — the kind found in faces, butterflies, and leaves. It’s the most common symmetry in the animal kingdom.
cycles_through_the_color_spectrum_as_y: cycles through the color spectrum as you draw, creating gradient-like mandala effects automatically.
the_quick_brown_fox_jumps_over_the_laz: The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog
sculpt_failed: Sculpting failed — try a simpler description.
sculpt_ai_label: Describe a sculpture or a change
maximum_contrast_pop_art_brand_accents: Maximum contrast. Pop art, brand accents.
vibrant_but_balanced_childrens_books_c: Vibrant but balanced. Childrens books, cartoons.
rich_palette_with_two_opposing_pairs: Rich palette with two opposing pairs.
adjust_base_hue_saturation_lightness_v: Adjust base hue, saturation, lightness variation, rotation, and palette size. Widget renders a live harmony palette and classifies it into one of 5 discrete harmony types. No score, no reveal — sweep and notice which combinations produce which harmonies.
hypothesis_free_text_no_right_answer_w: Hypothesis (free text — no right answer): What makes a palette feel harmonious vs jarring?
find_the_smallest_palette_that_still_f: Find the smallest palette that still feels "complete" to you.
real_impressionists_used_analogous_pal: Real impressionists used analogous palettes. Why might that be?
some_color_schemes_have_proper_names_c: Some color schemes have proper names (complementary, split-complementary, triadic). Look those up and try to reproduce them.
high_saturation_many_colors_busy_try_d: High saturation + many colors = busy. Try desaturating with the blend slider — what happens to "harmony"?
i_think_i_understand_color_harmony_now: I think I understand color harmony now — let me explain it in my own words
explain_in_your_own_words_how_do_hue_s: Explain in your own words: how do hue spacing, saturation, and palette size determine "harmony"?
design_note_discrete_5_state_harmony_m: Design note: discrete 5-state harmony marker; SVG wheel shows palette positions; no "good palette" score — by design.
the_path_traced_by_a_point_on_a_small_: — the path traced by a point on a small circle rolling inside a larger one. The pattern depends on the
greatest_common_divisor_of_the_two_rad: (greatest common divisor) of the two radii. When R/r is a simple fraction, you get fewer petals; complex ratios create intricate, never-repeating paths.
click_or_drag_on_the_canvas_to_create_: 👆 Click or drag on the canvas to create particle bursts
click_and_drag_to_drip_paint_on_the_sp: 👆 Click and drag to drip paint on the spinning canvas
in_a_spinning_reference_frame_objects_: In a spinning reference frame, objects experience an outward pseudo-force proportional to their distance from the center and the square of angular velocity (ω²r).
real_spin_art_uses_centripetal_acceler: Real spin art uses centripetal acceleration to spread paint. Thinner paint flies outward faster; thicker paint creates shorter, more controlled trails.
the_combination_of_rotational_motion_a: The combination of rotational motion and paint viscosity creates natural spirals and interference patterns. No two spin paintings are ever alike — it’s a form of
from_straight_lines_with_a_circle_and_: from straight lines. With a circle and multiplier of 2, you get a
the_heart_shaped_curve_seen_in_coffee_: — the heart-shaped curve seen in coffee cups. Multiplier 3 makes a
higher_multipliers_create_intricate_pa: . Higher multipliers create intricate patterns governed by
nail_n_connects_to_nail_n_m_mod_total: : nail N connects to nail (N × M) mod total.
optical_art_emerged_in_the_1960s_pione: (Optical Art) emerged in the 1960s, pioneered by
it_exploits_the_mechanics_of_human_vis: . It exploits the mechanics of human vision to create illusions of movement, vibration, and depth on flat surfaces.
appear_when_two_regular_grids_overlap_: appear when two regular grids overlap at slight angles. Your brain can’t resolve the conflicting patterns, creating phantom curves and waves. This same effect causes the “screen door” shimmer on some fabrics.
occur_when_highly_saturated_complement: occur when highly saturated complementary colors sit side by side. Your eye’s color receptors compete, creating a buzzing, unstable edge—this is called
in_the_retina_are_the_main_perceptual_: in the retina are the main perceptual mechanisms. Concentric ring patterns trigger involuntary eye saccades, making the artwork seem to breathe and pulse.
or_tiling_covers_a_plane_with_shapes_t: (or tiling) covers a plane with shapes that fit together without gaps or overlaps. Only three regular polygons tile by themselves:
1898_1972_transformed_simple_tilings_i: (1898–1972) transformed simple tilings into art by warping tile edges. His technique: deform one side of a shape and copy the deformation to the opposite side, so tiles still fit together perfectly. This is the basis of the
uses_tessellations_extensively_combini: uses tessellations extensively—combining stars, hexagons, and interlocking patterns seen in mosques like the Alhambra. These patterns follow strict mathematical rules while creating breathtaking visual complexity.
flip_every_tessellation_can_be_classif: (flip). Every tessellation can be classified by which of these 17 'wallpaper groups' it belongs to.
click_tiles_to_cycle_their_colors: 👆 Click tiles to cycle their colors
is_generated_by_iterating_z_z_c_for_ev: is generated by iterating z = z² + c for every point c in the complex plane. Points where |z| stays bounded (never exceeds 2) are 'in' the set. The boundary reveals
use_the_same_formula_but_fix_c_and_var: use the same formula but fix c and vary the starting z. Each Mandelbrot point generates a unique Julia set — points inside the Mandelbrot produce connected Julias; outside points produce dust-like 'Fatou sets'.
fractal_modifies_the_iteration_to_z_re: fractal modifies the iteration to z = (|Re(z)| + i|Im(z)|)² + c, creating an asymmetric shape resembling a flaming vessel. It was discovered by Michael Michelitsch and Otto Rössler in 1992.
is_built_by_the_chaos_game_pick_a_rand: is built by the 'chaos game': pick a random point, then repeatedly jump halfway toward a randomly chosen vertex. Remarkably, this random process produces a perfectly self-similar fractal.
1924_2010_coined_the_word_fractal_from: (1924–2010) coined the word 'fractal' from Latin 'fractus' (broken). He showed that coastlines, mountains, blood vessels, and stock markets all exhibit fractal geometry —
double_click_to_zoom_in_scroll_wheel_t: 👆 Double-click to zoom in • Scroll-wheel to zoom in/out
each_pixel_blends_red_green_and_blue_l: . Each pixel blends red, green, and blue light at different intensities. A gradient smoothly interpolates these values across space.
produces_more_perceptually_uniform_gra: produces more perceptually uniform gradients than RGB. Going from red to blue in RGB passes through muddy grays; in HSL, it sweeps through vivid purples — the way a painter would mix.
are_everywhere_sunsets_rayleigh_scatte: are everywhere: sunsets (Rayleigh scattering separates wavelengths), rainbows (refraction sorts light by frequency), and ocean depths (water absorbs red first, leaving deep blue).
sweep_color_around_a_center_point_like: sweep color around a center point like a color wheel. They’re used in pie charts, loading spinners, and data visualizations. CSS only added conic-gradient support in 2020!
use_gradients_to_create_depth_direct_a: use gradients to create depth, direct attention, and evoke emotion. Warm-to-cool gradients suggest depth (atmospheric perspective); light-to-dark suggests volume (chiaroscuro).
describe_an_object_for_a_depth_map_or_: Describe an object for a depth map or a texture for a pattern...
white_pops_out_gray_middle_black_far: White = pops out • Gray = middle • Black = far
so_each_sees_the_world_from_a_slightly: , so each sees the world from a slightly different angle. Your brain fuses these two views to perceive
magic_eye_images_hide_3d_shapes_in_a_r: (Magic Eye™ images) hide 3D shapes in a repeating pattern. The trick: where the hidden object is ‘close,’ the pattern repeats with a
where_it_s_far_the_period_is_longer_yo: ; where it’s ‘far,’ the period is longer. Your brain decodes these period differences as depth.
1993_94_by_tom_baccei_and_cheri_smith_: (1993–94) by Tom Baccei and Cheri Smith sold over 25 million copies worldwide. The underlying autostereogram technique was pioneered by Dr. Christopher Tyler in 1979.
to_see_the_image_hold_your_face_close_: 🧠 To see the image: hold your face close to the screen, relax your eyes as if looking ‘through’ the image at a distant wall, then slowly pull back. The 3D shape will ‘pop’ into view. This is called
have_difficulty_seeing_stereograms_due: have difficulty seeing stereograms due to conditions like amblyopia (lazy eye), strabismus (crossed eyes), or other binocular vision differences. This is completely normal!
relax_your_eyes_and_look_through_the_i: Relax your eyes and look ‘through’ the image to see 3D
hold_your_face_close_to_the_screen: Hold your face close to the screen
relax_your_eyes_try_to_look_through_th: Relax your eyes — try to look ‘through’ the image at a wall behind it
slowly_move_back_a_3d_shape_will_emerg: Slowly move back. A 3D shape will emerge!
tip_the_two_guide_dots_above_should_ap: Tip: the two guide dots above should appear as three when your eyes are set correctly
create_animated_3d_stereograms_from_pr: Create animated 3D stereograms from presets, custom drawings, uploaded images, transforms, or AI-generated depth maps!
draw_a_depth_map_capture_it_as_a_keyfr: Draw a depth map, capture it as a keyframe, then draw the next. The animation will interpolate between them.
upload_a_grayscale_image_white_near_bl: Upload a grayscale image (white = near, black = far). It will be animated using the selected transform.
animates_the_depth_map_from_the_static: Animates the depth map from the Static tab using a chosen transform effect. Switch to Static mode first to draw your depth map.
tip_draw_a_depth_map_in_the_static_tab: 💡 Tip: Draw a depth map in the Static tab first, then come back here to animate it with a transform.
describe_a_3d_scene_and_ai_will_genera: Describe a 3D scene and AI will generate a depth map, then animate it with a transform.
e_g_a_glowing_crystal_orb_floating_in_: e.g. A glowing crystal orb floating in space...
generate_ai_depth_map_for_animation: Generate AI Depth Map for Animation
ai_motion_calls_gemini_to_plan_poses_t: AI Motion calls Gemini to plan poses then Imagen to render each frame as its own depth map. ~5–7s per frame; rate-limit-safe.
ai_image_generation_is_not_available_u: ⚠️ AI image generation is not available. Use the Preset, Draw, Upload, or Transform modes instead.
relax_your_eyes_and_look_through_the_a: Relax your eyes and look ‘through’ the animation to see 3D shapes move
lock_your_eyes_into_the_3d_view_before: Lock your eyes into the 3D view before clicking Play
slower_speeds_4_6_fps_are_easier_to_ma: Slower speeds (4–6 FPS) are easier to maintain focus
pulse_and_heart_presets_are_the_easies: Pulse and Heart presets are the easiest to see in motion
the_exported_gif_can_be_printed_frame_: The exported GIF can be printed frame-by-frame as a flipbook!
